records.ety: [Sc. also bratchart; fr. ME. brachet, fr. OF. brachet; ML. brachetus, dim. of brache a hound. See brach.]

defs: a kind of hound; a brach; -- applied contemptuously to a child. See also brach.
quotes: The bratchet's bay From the dark covert drove the prey.
To be plagued with a bratchet whelp -- Whence came ye, my fair-favoured little gossip? .
